tax: Placed at genus rank, Roiler is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a molten-basalt flowing body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a molten lava body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a roiling crust-skinned lava lobe. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a cooled basalt lobe and glazed track — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Roiler occupies lava benches and vents. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where vent heat melts basalt and recur with the vent reheats the cooled lobe, sustaining themselves on thermal-vent heat.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: flows to engulf and radiates heat. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: molten body and glowing-crack core. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: engulfing and heat-radiation. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Roiler matters because lethal by radiant heat alone. It lets survey reports sort flowing molten forms from glassy ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Roiler is the genus-rank grouping of flowing molten-basalt elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a roiling lava body that engulfs and radiates heat, and divide below into species groups by lava lobe size and vent strength. A self-contained flow of living lava.
